resapp.description: A vehicle booking system for managing fleet reservations with real-time availability.
resapp.summary: A full-stack vehicle booking system for managing municipal fleet reservations with real-time availability.
resapp.problem: City employees needed to book municipal vehicles, but the process was entirely manual — paper forms, phone calls, and no visibility into availability.
resapp.solution: A web application that lets employees book vehicles with live availability, view their upcoming reservations, and receive email confirmations. Administrators can manage the fleet and view utilization stats.
resapp.challenges: Handling concurrent bookings without race conditions was the main technical challenge — solved with PostgreSQL advisory locks to prevent double-booking.

equipmentTracker.description: Internal tool for tracking IT equipment provisioning across departments.
equipmentTracker.summary: Internal tool for tracking IT equipment provisioning across municipal departments.
equipmentTracker.problem: The IT service had no centralized way to track which equipment was assigned to which department or employee — it was managed through scattered spreadsheets.
equipmentTracker.solution: A lightweight CRUD application for logging equipment assignments, tracking returns, and viewing status history per item. Currently in active development.
equipmentTracker.challenges: Designing the data model to handle partial provisioning — where a single item (like a batch of cables) can be split across multiple departments — without losing traceability.

goalTracker.description: A lightweight app for setting and tracking personal goals.
goalTracker.summary: A lightweight app for setting, tracking, and reflecting on personal goals.
goalTracker.problem: I wanted a simple way to track personal goals without signing up for a complex productivity suite.
goalTracker.solution: A small React app backed by Firebase for persistence. Deliberately minimal — just what's needed, nothing more.
goalTracker.challenges: Keeping scope intentionally small — resisting the pull toward feature creep and building only what was actually useful.

about.title: About
about.bio: IT student in BTS CIEL IR2, working at a townhall IT service. Passionate about full-stack development and polished user experiences. Bilingual French & English with strong hardware and systems knowledge.
